title: Local Daemon Implementation Contract Evidence
purpose: Proves the public daemon runtime scheduling contract from executable code references instead of hand-written prose.

facts.name: daemon exposes local task sources by env
facts.summary: cmd/riido owns the local queue and task DB source environment surface.

facts.name: supervisor owns runtime pool dispatch
facts.summary: daemon startup wires runtime actors into SupervisorActor and supervisor claims work by runtime id.

facts.name: supervisor gates provider start on accepted start report
facts.summary: provider preparation and execution do not begin when StartTask reporting is rejected.

facts.name: supervisor retains terminal evidence until accepted
facts.summary: terminal task results remain in actor state and are retried when CompleteTask reporting is rejected.

facts.name: supervisor retries low-volume state event evidence
facts.summary: stateful report events are retained and retried before terminal reports, while stream deltas stay out of the retry queue.

facts.name: scheduler selection is pure C5 logic
facts.summary: SelectRuntime orders candidates without locks, persistence, provider execution, or process side effects.

facts.name: task DB uses sidecar runtime registries
facts.summary: taskdbplane stores runtime registry and lease registry next to the task DB without changing the task DB schema.

facts.name: task DB claims only the selected runtime
facts.summary: taskdbplane reuses SelectRuntime and only lets the selected runtime id claim the queued row.

facts.name: lease metadata fences reports
facts.summary: claimed TaskRequest metadata carries lease id, fencing token, and capability fingerprint; reports must match active lease identity.

facts.name: expired leases requeue or terminate by state
facts.summary: expired Preparing/Running leases hand off through Blocked then Queued, while Claimed and NeedsInput become terminal.

facts.name: heartbeats refresh only matching active leases
facts.summary: runtime heartbeats refresh running task leases only when runtime id and capability fingerprint still match.

boundaries.name: internal/scheduling
boundaries.summary: pure runtime eligibility, selection, and runtime pin model

boundaries.name: internal/agentbridge/supervisor
boundaries.summary: runtime registration, claim dispatch, heartbeat, cancellation, and pre-submit eligibility

boundaries.name: internal/agentbridge/controlplane/taskdbplane
boundaries.summary: task DB source/reporter, runtime registry sidecars, leases, fencing, and guarded task transitions

boundaries.name: cmd/riido daemon
boundaries.summary: env/flag surface and runtime actor pool wiring

absent_surfaces.name: scheduler has no adapter or persistence imports
absent_surfaces.reason: C5 scheduling must stay pure and must not own daemon adapter, task DB, lease primitive, or HTTP behavior.

absent_surfaces.name: taskdbplane has no provider execution imports
absent_surfaces.reason: taskdbplane may claim/report tasks but must not execute provider processes.

source_checks.name: scheduler-pure-selector
source_checks.contains: provider execution side effects happen here

source_checks.name: scheduler-runtime-pin
source_checks.contains: The (RuntimeID, CapabilityFingerprint) pair IS the runtime pin

source_checks.name: taskdb-expired-claimed-fails
source_checks.contains: runtime lease expired before provider execution

source_checks.name: taskdb-expired-needs-input-times-out
source_checks.contains: runtime lease expired while waiting for input

assertions: local daemon scheduling claims are runtime-scoped, not process-global
scheduler selection is pure and cannot acquire leases or spawn providers
task DB runtime state is sidecar state, not a task DB schema expansion
progress/result reports require active lease identity and fencing token evidence
expired leases have explicit handoff or terminal outcomes
